_provenance.method: FAO-56 Chapter 7 + Chapter 11 — Cover crop and no-till Kc adaptation
references: Allen et al. (1998) FAO-56 Table 17 + Ch 11: Kc for non-pristine conditions
Islam et al. (2014) No-till and conservation agriculture: David Brandt farm, ISWCR
USDA NRCS Cover Crop Termination Guidelines
_provenance.reproduction_note: Re-run baseline_command at baseline_commit to regenerate expected values

cover_crop_kcb._description: Basal Kc for common cover crops in no-till systems. Based on FAO-56 Table 17 + literature adjustments for cover crop growth habits.

cereal_rye._note: Winter cereal rye — dominant Midwest cover crop. Kcb_mid reflects full cover before spring termination.

crimson_clover._note: Legume cover — lower max height, moderate transpiration.

winter_wheat_cover._note: Same as winter wheat but terminated early for cover crop use.

hairy_vetch._note: Vining legume — moderate transpiration, good ground cover.

radish_tillage._note: Daikon/tillage radish — winterkills, acts as green mulch.

no_till_mulch_effects._description: No-till surface residue reduces soil evaporation by limiting energy reaching the soil surface. This reduces Ke and can reduce Kc_ini by 25-50%.
no_till_mulch_effects._equation: Ke_mulch = Ke × mulch_factor; net effect: Kc_ini_adj ≈ Kcb_ini + Ke × mulch_factor

no_till_soil_moisture._description: No-till soil moisture observations from Islam et al. (2014) — David Brandt farm, Central Ohio. 40+ years of no-till conservation agriculture.

transition_scenarios._description: Growing season transition: cover crop → cash crop in no-till system

rye_to_corn._note: Cereal rye terminated at corn planting (early May), corn planted into rye mulch

phases._note: Rye terminated, corn just planted, heavy residue

mulch_reduces_evaporation._description: No-till residue should reduce Ke and thus reduce total ET during initial growth

no_till_conserves_water._description: No-till should result in lower seasonal ET (due to reduced evaporation) and higher soil moisture

expected_et_reduction_pct._note: 5-15% full season; up to 50% during initial stage when Ke dominates
